summary: All code_refs and source figures verified against ground truth. Frame base fields, the three base categories (System/Data/Control), audio/transcription/LLM/TTS/lifecycle/boundary frame classes, num_frames=len(audio)/(num_channels*2), and the prior-art divergence claims (Modal generic frames, Cloudflare JSON protocol messages) all match. Two citations (LLMTextFrame:332, StartFrame:838) point at the @dataclass decorator line one above the class statement — standard practice, treated as SUPPORTED.

claims.claim: Frame base has id, name, pts (ns), metadata, transport_source/destination
claims.found: class Frame at 55; fields id/name/pts/metadata/transport_source/transport_destination at 73-79; pts 'in nanoseconds'

claims.claim: Three base categories SystemFrame/DataFrame/ControlFrame with interrupt semantics
claims.found: 95 SystemFrame ('not affected by user interruptions'), 106 DataFrame ('cancelled by user interruptions'), 118 ControlFrame

claims.claim: InputAudioRawFrame/UserAudioRawFrame are SystemFrames carrying PCM audio/sample_rate/num_channels
claims.found: 1227 class InputAudioRawFrame(SystemFrame, AudioRawFrame); 1273 UserAudioRawFrame(InputAudioRawFrame); AudioRawFrame fields audio:bytes/sample_rate/num_channels at 161-163

claims.claim: TranscriptionFrame (final) and InterimTranscriptionFrame (partial) both subclass TextFrame->DataFrame

claims.found: 332 @dataclass / 333 class LLMTextFrame(TextFrame) ('Text frame generated by LLM services'); cite points at decorator line
963 UserStartedSpeakingFrame(SystemFrame); 974 UserStoppedSpeakingFrame(SystemFrame); 962 is @dataclass decorator (also frontmatter code_ref :962)
838 @dataclass / 839 class StartFrame(SystemFrame) ('Initial frame to start pipeline processing'); cite points at decorator line

claims.claim: num_frames derived as len(audio)/(num_channels*2) assuming 16-bit PCM

claims.found: line 16: 'Pipeline coordinates a series of processors handling real-time audio/text/video frames with ultra-low latency'

claims.claim: Cloudflare voice mixin uses plain JSON protocol messages (transcript, status, playback_interrupt) over WebSocket, not frame types
claims.found: 586-594 #sendJSON(connection,{type:'status',status:'listening'}) and {type:'playback_interrupt'}; type:'transcript' at 611; #sendJSON wraps sendVoiceJSON over connection
